topic: Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry — post-patch verification (c6 → c7)
change_summary: Post-patch verification: c6 flagged 1 major + 4 moderate + 3 minor holes. Verifying whether ISS-1949 (denominator) / ISS-1950 (IC framing) patches landed, whether ISS-1948 (agonic-strawman steelman paragraph) survives reframing, whether c6 holes #3 (metric-type) and #4 (v22 self-own) text now reads correctly, and fresh-eyes attack on the new text.
our_argument_summary: Bermuda/Devil's Sea 'geometric symmetry CONFIRMED' fails on three grounds: (a) the disappearance claim is independently refuted by NOAA/Lloyd's/USCG; (b) under the quantitative 180° antipodal reading the gap is 28° (15.6%, ~3× the dome's own 5.3% WIN-029 threshold); (c) under the vague reading the claim is unfalsifiable; the agonic-line mechanism (the only plausible standard physics steelman) does not run through either region.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No pipeline. WIN-028 is a single-line published assertion ('Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED') with no formula, no tolerance, no mechanism, and no monitor.py entry. The v50.4 evolution log adds WIN-028 alongside WIN-027 but provides no derivation. The dome's automation suite never touches this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — the claim is pure assertion, not validated against any data source.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The lack of a published tolerance is itself the escape clause: any pair of same-latitude points anywhere on Earth could plausibly be argued to 'confirm' the WIN under a vague reading.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The detail_verdict_text correctly states 'No monitor.py entry exists for this WIN.' Re-verified: no monitor.py exists anywhere in the repo (find . -name monitor.py returns empty); grep for 'bermuda', 'devil', 'symmetry' across raw-text/ returns only the WIN list entry and one evolution log line.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Re-verified at c7: monitor.py is absent from the repo entirely. grep across raw-text/ for 'bermuda', 'devil', 'agonic', 'symmetry' returns only WIN-028's title line in 02-wins.txt:113 and its v50.4 evolution-log entry. Unchanged from c5/c6.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Bermuda Triangle / Devil's Sea 'disappearance zone' framing is paranormal folklore, not standard physics. Agonic lines are real geophysics but the dome's WIN-028 text doesn't actually invoke them — our review imports the agonic-line idea as a charitable steelman for the dome's unspecified mechanism. No standard-physics phenomenon is being relabeled. Unchanged from c6.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Bermuda Triangle: Gaddis 1964 (Argosy), Berlitz 1974 (book). Devil's Sea folklore: Edo-period Japan, popularised by Berlitz 1974. Both regions' coordinates were established on nautical charts centuries before the dome model. Dome v50.4 added WIN-028 on 2026-03-09 (raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44). Pure retrodiction of pre-existing folklore. The detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph correctly grounds this in the dome author's own timestamping definition.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site connects disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, or any other dome parameter to the longitudes of Bermuda or Devil's Sea. Re-verified at c7 — no derivation, no parameter dependence, no quantitative tolerance.

patches_verified_landed.c6_hole: H0 major — agonic strawman in 'The mechanism' paragraph
patches_verified_landed.evidence: Current detail_evidence reads: 'The mechanism (steelman test). The dome publishes no mechanism for the claimed symmetry. We test the only standard physical mechanism that could plausibly link two folklore zones at roughly the same latitude across hemispheres — agonic-line correlation — as our charitable steelman.' This is the verbatim dual-reading-frame fix c6 H0 recommended (the same patch shape successfully applied to the 180° strawman in c5/EXP-298). Strawman risk substantively closed; only the heading 'The mechanism (steelman test)' remains as a residual minor ambiguity (see hole #4 below).

Current detail_evidence reads: '(relative to the target of 180° — the dome's claim is antipodal symmetry so the target is 180°; relative error = deviation ÷ target is the standard form — no other referent is published by the dome).' This anchors the 28/180 choice cleanly. Hole closed.

Current detail_evidence reads: '...we do not endorse 5.3% as a meaningful tolerance (WIN-029 is itself verdicted Self-Contradicted in our review), but cite it here as an internal-consistency benchmark only — showing the dome's own framework treats single-digit residuals as confirmation — not as an independently valid standard.' This is exactly the c5/c6 reframing recommendation. Hole closed.

patches_verified_landed.c6_hole: H3 moderate — metric-type conflation (R², RMS, residual)
patches_verified_landed.evidence: Current detail_evidence reads: 'WIN-027 reports R² = 0.7874 for a fitted polynomial as confirmation; WIN-030 cites a 48.6% RMS reduction (these are distinct statistical measures from a residual percentage, but each reflects the dome accepting tight fits as its standard). A 15.6% residual is roughly three times the WIN-029 5.3% residual benchmark.' The like-for-like comparator is now explicitly WIN-029 (residual % to residual %), and the R²/RMS cites are bracketed as a separate looser-confirmation pattern. Hole closed.

patches_verified_landed.c6_hole: H4 moderate — v22 self-own (100% deviation vs. 15.6%)
patches_verified_landed.evidence: Current detail_evidence no longer mentions v22 anywhere in the quantitative-reading paragraph. The internal-consistency case now rests solely on the like-for-like WIN-029 5.3% comparator, exactly as c6 H4 recommended. Hole closed.

patches_outstanding_in_tracker: ISS-1933, 1934, 1935, 1936, 1937, 1948, 1949, 1950, 1951, 1952 are all status=open in the issue tracker per ${CLONE}/monitor/decisions/open-issues.json, but the textual fixes c6 recommended for each have landed in data/wins.json's WIN-028 detail_evidence (verified above). This appears to be a tracker-bookkeeping lag, not a patch failure. The decider's next M1 sweep should close these as fixed-pending-verification, citing this c7 review as the verification record.

holes_found.description: TLDR-vs-detail tension. tldr_evidence reads 'The dome's claimed Bermuda/Japan symmetry has no factual basis.' But the detail_evidence kernel paragraph explicitly concedes 'The two folklore zones do sit at roughly opposite hemispheres in this loose sense, and that is a real geographic observation.' A hostile defender lands the easy hit: 'Your headline says no factual basis; your detail concedes a real geographic observation. Which is it?' The TLDR is what most readers see (it is the visible sentence before the <details> expansion). Per curmudgeon.md TLDR Review: TLDR factual tension with detail is at the major boundary. Rated moderate rather than major because 'no factual basis' is defensible if narrowly read as 'no basis for the dome's stronger Geometric-Symmetry-with-mechanism-and-disappearance-pattern claim' — but a casual reader does not parse that nuance.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite tldr_evidence to acknowledge the kernel while making the verdict clear. Suggested: 'The longitudes of the Bermuda Triangle and Devil's Sea really do sit near opposite sides of the globe — but 28° (15.6%) short of true antipodal, well outside the dome's own confirmation thresholds. NOAA, Lloyd's, and the U.S. Coast Guard confirm no anomalous loss rates in either region; the dome publishes no tolerance or mechanism that would convert this near-coincidence into a CONFIRMED prediction.' This compresses the three-part case (kernel concession + quantitative miss + disappearance refutation) into the TLDR length budget.

holes_found.description: Summary-table 'finding' field doesn't represent the strongest argument. Current finding: 'NOAA and Lloyd's confirm no anomalous loss rates in either region.' This is the disappearance-claim refutation only. The detail_evidence's central quantitative argument — the 28° gap is a 15.6% deviation, ~3× the dome's own 5.3% WIN-029 confirmation threshold — does not appear in the summary row. A reader scanning the WIN table sees a verdict of 'Refuted by Data' but a finding that addresses only half the case; a defender could say 'Your summary row is about disappearance rates, not about symmetry — so your verdict on the symmetry claim rests on nothing in the summary view.' Per curmudgeon.md per-WIN step 1: 'A one-liner that overstates, understates, or mischaracterizes the detail evidence is a hole a defender can exploit — readers may never click through to the detail.'
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite finding to combine both legs of the argument. Suggested: '28° gap from antipodal symmetry (15.6%, ~3× the dome's own 5.3% threshold); NOAA and Lloyd's confirm no anomalous loss rates.' This puts the quantitative miss in the visible row and preserves the disappearance-data leg as a secondary clause.

holes_found.description: 'Opposite hemispheres in this loose sense' wording. The kernel paragraph reads: 'The two folklore zones do sit at roughly opposite hemispheres in this loose sense.' But both Bermuda Triangle (~25°N) and Devil's Sea (~25°N) are in the Northern Hemisphere. They are not in opposite hemispheres in any standard usage of the word. The intended meaning is 'opposite sides of the globe longitudinally' or 'near-antipodal in longitude' — which the next clause already makes explicit. The 'hemispheres' word here is loose to the point of being wrong, and a pedantic defender will jump on it.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'opposite hemispheres' with 'opposite sides of the globe' or 'near-opposite longitudes' (the latter is already used two sentences earlier — the kernel paragraph could simply drop the redundant 'opposite hemispheres' clause: '...that is the measure the dome's claim invokes.) The two folklore zones do sit at near-opposite longitudes, and that is a real geographic observation.'

holes_found.description: Residual ambiguity in 'The mechanism (steelman test)' heading. The current text says 'The dome publishes no mechanism for the claimed symmetry. We test the only standard physical mechanism... as our charitable steelman.' The body now does the dual-reading-frame work correctly (ISS-1933/1948 substantively closed). But the bold heading 'The mechanism (steelman test).' can be misread by a hostile defender as implying the dome HAS a mechanism we are testing. The (steelman test) parenthetical is doing all the work; without it, the heading would attribute a mechanism the dome does not publish. The heading is functional but suboptimal.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the heading to make the steelman framing leading rather than parenthetical. Suggested: 'Steelman: the strongest available mechanism (none published by the dome).' Or: 'The strongest standard-physics mechanism (a steelman we supply, not the dome's).' Low priority — body text is correct, heading is the residual.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER from c6 H5 (ISS-1938 / 1941 / 1954): Devil's Sea latitude stated as '~25°N, 137°E' without anchoring. The Devil's Sea / Dragon's Triangle in folklore spans 25–35°N (Berlitz 1974 places the centroid south of Honshu extending toward Iwo Jima and Guam — central estimate ~30°N, not 25°N). Picking 25°N matches the Bermuda Triangle's 25°N latitude and frames as longitudinal-symmetry-at-equal-latitude, but at the more typical Devil's Sea centroid the latitude match weakens. Still open in current text — neither anchored nor replaced.
holes_found.recommendation: Either anchor: 'we use 25°N as a central estimate; folklore variants span 25–35°N (per Berlitz 1974)' — or shift the argument to longitude-only ('two folklore zones at mid-northern latitudes, separated by ~152° in longitude'), which is cleaner and doesn't depend on the latitude pick.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER from c6 H6 (ISS-1939 / 1942 / 1955): the vague-reading paragraph says 'any pair of points within roughly 50° of antipodal could pass.' The 50° is unsourced and appears reverse-engineered. A defender will ask: why 50°, not 30° or 70°? If we had said 30°, the 28° actual gap would pass and our argument would fail. Still open in current text.
holes_found.recommendation: Either anchor the number (with a folklore-pair distribution argument) or — preferably — replace with the stronger pure-unfalsifiability framing: 'Without a published threshold, any deviation up to 180° (i.e., any pair of points at all) could pass, and the dome publishes no rule for which pairs do not.' This makes the unfalsifiability bite without needing the suspicious 50°.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER from c6 H7 (ISS-1940 / 1943 / 1956): 'Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025' is not citation-anchored. WMM 2025 is real (NOAA/NCEI/British Geological Survey, released December 2024, covers 2025–2030) and the cited declination positions (western agonic 95–100°W, eastern 100–120°E) are correct, but the citation should carry a URL. Still open in current text.
holes_found.recommendation: Add inline URL on first mention: 'Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025, the western agonic currently runs near 95–100°W...'

stronger_arguments.description: Triple-reading rather than dual-reading. The dome's unspecified 'Geometric Symmetry' has at least three readings, all of which fail: (a) latitudinal-only symmetry (both regions at ~25°N, exact match — but trivially true for any same-latitude pair on Earth, hence vacuous as a prediction); (b) longitudinal-antipodal symmetry (28° miss, 15.6%, fails the dome's own 5.3% threshold); (c) underspecified (any other interpretation), hence unfalsifiable. The current text addresses (b) and (c) but elides (a). Adding the (a) leg would pre-empt the latitudinal-equivalence pivot — a defender could currently say 'the symmetry is the matched latitude, not the antipodal longitude' and the current text doesn't fully close that exit (the agonic-mechanism paragraph implicitly assumes the latitudinal observation as the kernel without explicitly ruling out 'matched latitude IS the symmetry' as the dome's intended reading).
sources: This c7 review's analysis; dome raw-text/02-wins.txt:113

stronger_arguments.description: Quantify the 'roughly opposite hemispheres in this loose sense' concession more precisely. The two regions are at the same latitude (~25°N) but their longitudes differ by 152°/208° (28° off antipodal). Calling this 'roughly opposite hemispheres' is geographically loose. The cleaner concession is: 'The two regions sit at near-opposite longitudes (~152° apart, 28° short of true 180°), at matched mid-northern latitudes.' This frames the kernel of truth precisely.

kernel_of_truth.description: The longitudinal near-antipodal observation is real (data/wins.json:WIN-028 detail_evidence kernel paragraph). Bermuda Triangle (~25°N, 71°W) and Devil's Sea (~25°N, 137°E) differ by 152°/208° in longitude (great-circle distance ~123°), 28° short of true antipodal. Both regions also share a matched mid-northern latitude (~25°N), which is itself a real geographic fact. The dome model has identified a coincidence of folklore-zone placement that is genuinely worth noticing.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Coincidences in folklore-zone placement do not constitute prospective predictions. The dome adopted pre-existing 19th-/20th-century folklore (data/wins.json:WIN-028 detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph; raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 — single-line confirmation post date 2026-03-09 per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44) and labeled the coincidence CONFIRMED. The coincidence (a) does not match the dome's own thresholds for confirmation (15.6% vs WIN-029's 5.3%), (b) has no published mechanism (the only standard candidate, agonic-line correlation, fails quantitatively per IGRF-13/WMM 2025 — both agonic lines miss their associated regions by 17°–37°), and (c) does not produce the disappearance-rate signal the dome WIN's title requires (NOAA, Lloyd's, USCG all confirm no anomalous loss rates). A noticed coincidence is not a forecast, and the dome's CONFIRMED status requires a forecast under its own published methodology.

deeper_analysis:
The c6 → current diff added ~686 chars to detail_evidence (4778 → 5464), exactly the size budget needed for: (i) the denominator-anchor parenthetical (~150 chars), (ii) the IC-framing caveat clause (~250 chars), (iii) the metric-type parenthetical and like-for-like recentering on WIN-029 5.3% (~150 chars), and (iv) the v22 cite removal (net negative, ~ -100 chars) plus the dual-reading-frame steelman paragraph rebuild (~250 chars). All five c6 moderate+major holes are textually addressed. The remaining open vector is bookkeeping (tracker hasn't closed ISS-1933..1937 / 1948..1952) plus three c6 minor carry-overs (H5/H6/H7 still open in current text) plus two new moderates I am surfacing at c7 (TLDR overreach, summary-row underrepresentation of the quantitative case).

The substantive verdict (Refuted by Data) is robust and not in dispute. The remaining work is polish: TLDR alignment with the detail's kernel concession, summary-row inclusion of the 15.6% miss, three minor citation/anchoring fixes carried from c6, and one stylistic minor (the 'opposite hemispheres' wording).

The heading 'The mechanism (steelman test)' is the residual of ISS-1948. The body text now does what c6 H0 recommended — the strawman risk is closed by the dual-reading-frame fix in the paragraph itself. The heading is the last vestige; replacing it with a more explicit steelman framing would close ISS-1948 fully and remove the last attack surface on this paragraph.

No new attack against the verdict survives fresh examination. The defender pivot space has been compressed by EXP-298 + the c6-driven patches to: (1) the unanchored Devil's Sea latitude (c7-H5), (2) the unsourced 50° vague-tolerance (c7-H6), (3) the TLDR overreach (c7-H1), and (4) the summary-row coverage gap (c7-H2). None of these threaten the verdict; all are framing improvements.

Decider M1 sweep should close ISS-1933, 1934, 1935, 1936, 1937, 1948, 1949, 1950, 1951, 1952 as fixed-pending-verification, citing this c7 review. The three c6 minor duplicates (1938+1941+1954 = same Devil's Sea hole; 1939+1942+1955 = same 50° hole; 1940+1943+1956 = same WMM URL hole) should be consolidated into single open issues each and either patched or carried; current state of nine open minors when there are only three distinct issues is tracker churn.

citations_verified: NOAA Bermuda Triangle fact page: https://oceanservice.noaa.gov/facts/bermudatri.html — URL resolves, NOAA officially states no evidence of anomalous disappearances
WMM 2025: real model, released December 2024 by NOAA/NCEI/BGS, covers 2025-2030 — cited declination positions (western agonic 95-100°W, eastern 100-120°E) are correct
IGRF-13: real, latest revision of International Geomagnetic Reference Field
Internal cross-refs to WIN-027 (R²=0.7874), WIN-029 (5.3%), WIN-030 (48.6% RMS): match wins.json fields
Great-circle distance computation: cos(d) = sin²(25°) + cos²(25°)·cos(152°) ≈ -0.5467 → d ≈ 123.1° — matches the detail_evidence claim of '~123°'
citations_unchecked: Berlitz 1974 (Devil's Sea / Bermuda Triangle book): not directly verified but well-known reference
Gaddis 1964 (Argosy magazine 'Bermuda Triangle' coining): not directly verified but well-known

summary_for_decider:
VERDICT HOLDS (Refuted by Data). c6 → c7 verification: all five c6 major+moderate holes (H0 agonic-strawman, H1 denominator, H2 IC-framing, H3 metric-type, H4 v22 self-own) are textually closed by patches landed since c6 (detail_evidence length 4778 → 5464, +686 chars matches the expected patch budget). The three c6 minors (H5 Devil's Sea latitude, H6 50° vague-tolerance, H7 WMM 2025 URL) are still open in current text; tracked as c7-H5/H6/H7 with unresolved_prior_cycle entries pointing to ISS-1938/1941/1954, ISS-1939/1942/1955, ISS-1940/1943/1956 (three distinct holes spread across nine open tracker issues — consolidation opportunity).

TWO NEW MODERATES surfaced at c7: (1) tldr_evidence 'no factual basis' overreaches given detail's kernel-of-truth concession of the 'real geographic observation' — TLDR-detail tension that a defender can exploit; (2) summary-row finding 'NOAA and Lloyd's confirm no anomalous loss rates' covers only the disappearance-refutation leg, omitting the central quantitative argument (28° gap, 15.6%, ~3× dome's own threshold). Both are framing fixes, neither threatens the verdict. Recommended single-patch rewrites in c7-H1 and c7-H2.

TWO MINORS new at c7: (3) 'opposite hemispheres in this loose sense' is wrong wording — both regions are in northern hemisphere (c7-H3); (4) the 'The mechanism (steelman test)' heading is the residual of ISS-1948 — body text correctly does the steelman framing, but the heading alone could be misread as attributing a mechanism to the dome (c7-H4).

Decider actions recommended:
- Close as fixed-pending-verification (citing this c7): ISS-1933, 1934, 1935, 1936, 1937, 1948, 1949, 1950, 1951, 1952 (10 issues — all text closed in current state).
- Consolidate duplicate minor pairs: ISS-1938+1941+1954 → single; ISS-1939+1942+1955 → single; ISS-1940+1943+1956 → single.
- Self-apply c7-H1 (TLDR rewrite — single field replacement in tldr_evidence) and c7-H2 (finding rewrite — single field replacement). Both are mechanical text-only patches; ~30-60 word changes each.
- Self-apply c7-H3 (one-word stylistic fix, drop 'opposite hemispheres in this loose sense' or replace with 'near-opposite longitudes') and c7-H4 (heading rewrite, one phrase).
- Self-apply c7-H6 (replace the unsourced '50° of antipodal' with the stronger pure-unfalsifiability framing — the recommended replacement does not require new sourcing).
- Assign to analyst: c7-H5 (Devil's Sea latitude anchoring needs folklore-distribution research) — moderate research, not mechanical.
- Self-apply c7-H7 (add WMM 2025 URL — single inline link).

Queue 332 status: review complete. Recommend popping queue_id=332 and clearing the queue position. No follow-up cN+1 needed in priority queue unless the decider self-applies new text changes; the open c7 holes can route through ordinary M1/M2 work.

Verdict (Refuted by Data) confidence remains high. The dome's WIN-028 fails on three independent grounds (quantitative miss, unfalsifiability, refuted disappearance pattern) and the agonic-mechanism steelman doesn't rescue it. Post-EXP-298 + c6-patch state is in solid shape; remaining edits are polish, not substance.
